metadata.title:
WOMEN ARE IN


EARNEST

WILL MAKE HOUSE TO HOUSE
CAMPAIGN OF EDUCATION IN
SPOKANE FOR EQUAL SUI.
ERAGE CAUSE.

pages.raw_text:
A house to house campaign of
education in behalf of the right OF
suffrage for women was resolved
upon at the meeting of the Spo-
kane Equal Suffrage league last
night. Three parlor meetings and
one general meeting will be held
each month-one every week.


Mrs. May Arkwright Hutton,
PRESIDENT of the league, reported on
the success of the women's lobby
for equal suffrage at Olympia in
securing the submission of con-
stitutional amendment for that
purpose. She said that it was
found necessary to first convert
the wives of the legislators and
the rest was easy. The success of
woman uffrage in Washington de-
pended on the women themselves,
she said, as the men will grant it
II they think the women demand
the ballot.


Mrs. w. M. Wilson, E311S
Sprague avenue, denounced the ac-
ton of the legislators who defeated
the eight hour day for women. Mrs.
Wilson said that it was her mis-
fortune that she was compelled to
work in one of the laundries for
living and witnessed the spectacle
of her employer going to Olympia
to lobby against the eight hour
bill. If women voted she said men
would be compelled to earn the
living and the women who now
work could then devote some of
. their time to their families.


Mrs. Lydia Irons, 02023 Wash
ington street, told of the injustices
In property matters which women
rest under in some states. She
said she had worked in the suffrag-
list movement in Idaho and Oregon
and urged the formation of pre-
cinct organizations for the suffrag-
list campaign of 1910.


Other speakers were Mrs. Eliza
beth Godding, d. c. Coates of the
Labor World and Mrs. Hand, who
spoke of an encounter with an in
quisitive man assessor. 1t was de-
cided to cable the good wishes of
the club to Mrs. Baker of Spokane,
who has gone to London to repre-
sent the state of Washington in
the international suffragist conven-
ton in April.


Officers elected were: Presi-
dent, May Arkwright Hutton; vice
presidents, Lorene Baker, Mrs. w.
M. Wilson, Elizabeth Gibson; sec
rotary, Sadie Coates; treasurer,
Nellie Colbert.
